---
title: "happiness_project"
output: pdf_document
date: "2025-12-20"
---

```{r dataset}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
setwd("/Users/ilariamato/Desktop/DATA VIS")
a_cc <- read_excel("data_happiness.xlsx")
```

```{r  Rename Variables with clarification}
a <- a_cc %>%
  rename(
    `GDP per capita` = `Explained by: Log GDP per capita`,
    `Social Support` = `Explained by: Social support`,
    `Healthy life expectancy` = `Explained by: Healthy life expectancy`,
    `Freedom to make life choices` = `Explained by: Freedom to make life choices`,
    Generosity = `Explained by: Generosity`,
    `Perceptions of corruption` = `Explained by: Perceptions of corruption`,
    Happiness = `Life evaluation (3-year average)`,
    Country = `Country name`
  )

```


```{r  Missing values}
missing_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(a),
  Missing = colSums(is.na(a))
)

# Number of missing values
missing_summary <- missing_summary[order(-missing_summary$Missing), ]
print(missing_summary)

# Visualization of Missing
barplot(missing_summary$Missing, 
        names.arg = missing_summary$Variable,
        las = 2,  # Ruota etichette
        cex.names = 0.7,  # Dimensione nomi
        main = "Missing Values per Variabile",
        ylab = "Numero di Missing",
        col = "steelblue")

#List of missing 
vars_to_impute <- c(
  "Dystopia + residual",
  "Healthy life expectancy",           
  "Freedom to make life choices",     
  "Perceptions of corruption",         
  "GDP per capita",                   
  "Social Support",                   
  "Generosity"
)

#Imputazione con la media attraverso un ciclo FOR
for (var in vars_to_impute) {
  # Mean calculation (without NA)
  mean_value <- mean(a[[var]], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Put the mean instead of NA
  a[[var]][is.na(a[[var]])] <- mean_value
  
  # Info
  cat(sprintf("Imputato '%s' con media = %.4f\n", var, mean_value))
}

# to see if it worked
colSums(is.na(a))
```

```{r first: How has global happiness changed globally from 2019 to 2024?}
# Mean per year
global_trend <- a %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Happiness = mean(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),           
    Median_Happiness = median(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),       
    SD_Happiness = sd(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),               
    N_Countries = n()
  )
print(global_trend)

library(ggplot2)
# Temporal trend
ggplot(global_trend, aes(x = Year, y = Mean_Happiness)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.5, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "darkblue") +
  labs(title = "Evolution of Global Happiness (2019-2024)",
       y = "Average Happiness Score",
       x = "Year") +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r  second: How has global happiness changed by continent from 2019 to 2024?}
# Gruoping by continent
continent_mapping <- data.frame(
  Country = c(
    # Europa
    "Finland", "Denmark", "Iceland", "Sweden", "Norway", "Netherlands", 
    "Switzerland", "Luxembourg", "Austria", "Germany", "Belgium", "United Kingdom",
    "Ireland", "France", "Spain", "Italy", "Czech Republic", "Poland",
    
    # Asia
    "Israel", "United Arab Emirates", "Singapore", "Taiwan Province of China",
    "Japan", "South Korea", "Thailand", "Philippines", "Vietnam", "China",
    "India", "Pakistan", "Bangladesh", "Indonesia", "Malaysia",
    
    # Americas
    "Canada", "United States", "Mexico", "Brazil", "Argentina", "Chile",
    "Costa Rica", "Uruguay", "Panama", "Colombia", "Guatemala",
    
    # Oceania
    "Australia", "New Zealand",
    
    # Africa
    "Libya", "South Africa", "Morocco", "Algeria", "Egypt", "Nigeria",
    "Kenya", "Ghana", "Tanzania", "Uganda", "Zimbabwe"
  ),
  Continent = c(
    rep("Europe", 18),
    rep("Asia", 15),
    rep("Americas", 11),
    rep("Oceania", 2),
    rep("Africa", 11)
  )
)
# i have the most populous countries (capturing the majority of the world's population).
# i have countries with different GDPs (poor, middle-income, rich).
# i cover all macro-regions (Northern/Southern Europe, East/West Asia, etc.).
# Including both happy and unhappy countries.

# MERGE
a_with_continent <- a %>%
  left_join(continent_mapping, by = "Country") 

# Mean per year and continet
continent_trend <- a_with_continent %>%
  filter(!is.na(Continent)) %>%
  group_by(Year, Continent) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Happiness = mean(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE), 
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# viz continent mean
continent_summary <- a_with_continent %>%
  filter(!is.na(Continent)) %>%
  group_by(Year, Continent) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Happiness = mean(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median_Happiness = median(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Happiness = sd(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),
    N_Countries = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(continent_summary, n = Inf)

# ggplot for the means of continents
ggplot(continent_trend, aes(x = Year, y = Mean_Happiness, 
                            color = Continent, group = Continent)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Europe" = "#1f77b4",
    "Americas" = "#ff7f0e", 
    "Asia" = "#2ca02c",
    "Oceania" = "#d62728",
    "Africa" = "#9467bd"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Evolution of Happiness by Continent (2019-2024)",
    subtitle = "Comparison of regional trends",
    y = "Average Happiness Score",
    x = "Year",
    color = "Continent"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

```

```{r  third: Which countries analyzed have always been at the top of the rankings and which have always been at the bottom from 2019 to 2024?}

library(dplyr)

# ALWAYS IN TOP (2019-2024)
# top in every year
top_10_by_year <- a %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  arrange(Rank) %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  select(Year, Country, Rank)  

# How many times the country is in top 10
top_10_frequency <- top_10_by_year %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  # ⭐ CAMBIATO
  summarise(
    Times_in_Top10 = n(),
    Years = paste(Year, collapse = ", ")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Times_in_Top10))
print(top_10_frequency)

# Countries in top 10 for 6 years
always_top_10 <- top_10_frequency %>%
  filter(Times_in_Top10 == 6)
print(always_top_10)

# ALWAYS IN BOTTOM (2019-2024)
# bottom in every year
bottom_10_by_year <- a %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  arrange(desc(Rank)) %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  select(Year, Country, Rank)  

#  How many times the country is in bottom 10
bottom_10_frequency <- bottom_10_by_year %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%  
  summarise(
    Times_in_Bottom10 = n(),
    Years = paste(Year, collapse = ", ")
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Times_in_Bottom10))
print(bottom_10_frequency)

# Countries in top 10 for 6 years
always_bottom_10 <- bottom_10_frequency %>%
  filter(Times_in_Bottom10 == 6)
print(always_bottom_10)

```

```{r  forth: What is the GAP, or happiness gap, between the countries that consistently are top and bottom between 2019 and 2024?}
# Top vs Bottom for every year 
top_bottom_trend <- a %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  arrange(Rank) %>%
  mutate(
    Category = case_when(
      Rank <= 10 ~ "Top 10",
      Rank >= max(Rank) - 9 ~ "Bottom 10",
      TRUE ~ "Middle"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(Category != "Middle") %>%
  group_by(Year, Category) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Happiness = mean(Happiness, na.rm = TRUE),  # ⭐ CAMBIATO
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# GAP for every year
gap_trend <- top_bottom_trend %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Category, values_from = Mean_Happiness) %>%
  mutate(Gap = `Top 10` - `Bottom 10`)
print(gap_trend)

# gap_change (year-over-year changes)
gap_change <- data.frame(
  Period = c("2019-2020", "2020-2021", "2021-2022", "2022-2023", "2023-2024"),
  Change = c(
    gap_trend$Gap[2] - gap_trend$Gap[1],  # 2020 - 2019
    gap_trend$Gap[3] - gap_trend$Gap[2],  # 2021 - 2020
    gap_trend$Gap[4] - gap_trend$Gap[3],  # 2022 - 2021
    gap_trend$Gap[5] - gap_trend$Gap[4],  # 2023 - 2022
    gap_trend$Gap[6] - gap_trend$Gap[5]   # 2024 - 2023
  )
)
print(gap_change)

# Variation of gap ggplot
ggplot(gap_change, aes(x = Period, y = Change)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = Change > 0)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("TRUE" = "#BD0026", "FALSE" = "#008000"),
    labels = c("Gap decrease ↓", "Gap increase ↑")
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Year-over-Year Change in the Happiness Gap (2019-2024)",
    y = "Gap variation",
    x = "Year",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )


```

```{r  fifth: What are the global motivations for being happy and how are they related to happiness? How it changed over time? Are there differences between continents?}

correlation_data <- a %>%
  select(
    Happiness,
    `GDP per capita`,
    `Social Support`,
    `Healthy life expectancy`,
    `Freedom to make life choices`,
    Generosity,
    `Perceptions of corruption`
  )

# correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(correlation_data, use = "complete.obs")
print(round(cor_matrix, 3))

# viz
library(corrplot)
corrplot(cor_matrix, 
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.srt = 45,
         tl.cex = 0.8,  # ⭐ Riduci dimensione per nomi lunghi
         col = colorRampPalette(c(
           "#800026",
           "#BD0026",
           "#E31A1C",
           "#F03B20",
           "#FC6E4A",
           "#FD8D3C",
           "#FEB24C",
           "#FED976",
           "#FEEC8C",
           "#FFFFB2",
           "#FFFFE0"
         ))(200),
         title = "Correlations between Happiness Factors",
         mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0),
         addgrid.col = "gray90")

```
